channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Northern California gets record rain and heavy snow. Many have been in the dark for days in Seattle
item.description: A major storm with heavy snow and record rain that's moving through Northern California has toppled trees, closed roads and prompted evacuations in some areas after knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of people in Washington and Oregon.
item.dc:creator: Godofredo A. Vásquez, Janie Har And Christopher Weber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Twitch star rants against Israel, calls for another 9/11
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal shifts spotlight to Hegseth, Gabbard, Kennedy
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz's (R-Fla.) decision to withdraw from consideration to become attorney general has shifted the attention of Senate Republicans to the nominations of Pete Hegseth, former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Advocates fear national book ban under unified GOP government
item.description: A Republican trifecta in Washington is raising concerns over the potential for national book ban legislation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado dentist's murder trial put on hold after defense 'suddenly quit' prior to jury selection
item.description: A Colorado judge has delayed the trial for an Aurora dentist accused of poisoning his wife to death after the defense attorney suddenly quit during jury selection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas is taking a final vote on allowing Bible-infused lessons in public schools
item.description: Texas education officials will soon decide if a controversial Bible-infused curriculum can be taught in public schools.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Diddy private eyes confront influencers about possible payments
item.description: TikTokers Samson Crouppen and Amala Ekpunobi deny any pay arrangement and expressed fear and concern over the visits.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Police warn of sophisticated holiday scam artists targeting consumers
item.description: He said any time shoppers use their credit cards or personal information, they should go in with their eyes open.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Disneyland Paris turns into real-life winter wonderland after season's 1st snowfall
item.description: The season’s first snowfall has turned Disneyland Paris into a real-life winter wonderland. Disney Parks shared a video on Thursday showing snow covering Main Street, U.S.A, Sleeping Beauty Castle, and all the festive holiday décor. It’s currently pouring snow at Disneyland Paris. pic.twitter.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meghan Markle's bad week as Prince Harry outshines his wife
item.description: Across the pond in America, former British royal, Prince Harry, is making inroads to make himself likable again… and it’s working.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: US towns plunge into debates about fluoride in water
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For about 50 years, adding cavity-preventing fluoride to drinking water was a popular public health measure in Yorktown, a leafy town north of New York City. But in September, the town’s supervisor used his emergency powers to stop the practice.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia's Brian Kemp picked to lead Republican Governors Association
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will lead the Republican Governors Association for the next year, the group announced Wednesday, just weeks after the party secured governorships in eight out of 11 state races up for grabs in the election earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Whooping cough cases have quintupled. These states have it worst
item.description: Pertussis – a disease commonly referred to as whooping cough – is spreading quickly, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention warns. The U.S. has seen five times more cases in 2024 than we did at this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The DOJ’s proposal would require Google to divest from Chrome and prohibit the search giant from owning another browser for five years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trooper hit with car in Virginia Beach, suspect caught
item.description: A trooper is hurt after the driver of a blue Dodge Charger tried to flee in Virginia Beach on Wednesday morning, state police said.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indiana county commissioner pleads guilty to sexually assaulting daughter
item.description: Republican candidate John Jessup pleaded guilty to sexually assaulting his daughter in Las Vegas earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Progressive DA ousted for soft-on-crime policies spends last days in office helping 'Bob's Big Boy' killer
item.description: Outgoing LA DA George Gascon's office pushes resentencing for gunman on death row in 1980 Bob's Big Boy robbery that killed four and wounded four more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal Reserve's likely slowdown in rate cuts could disappoint borrowers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Just a few weeks ago, the path ahead for the Federal Reserve looked straightforward: With inflation cooling and the job market slowing, the Fed appeared on track to steadily cut interest rates.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CMA Awards: 2024 Winners | LIST
item.description: Keep up with all the biggest winners and moments from Country Music’s Biggest Night™ at the 58th Annual CMA Awards live from Nashville, TN!
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit against troopers who investigated 11-year-old in murder case nears trial
item.description: More than six years after he was exonerated based on insufficient evidence, a man who was charged as an 11-year-old with shooting his father's pregnant fiancee to death wants a federal jury to make the Pennsylvania State Police pay for the years he spent in juvenile detention.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man arrested, charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange: FBI
item.description: According to the FBI, a Florida man has been arrested and charged in a plot to bomb the New York Stock Exchange this week.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he was separated from a family hours before they froze to death
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — An Indian national who survived a treacherous trek across the U.S.-Canada border in blizzard conditions testified Wednesday that he got separated from a family of four shortly before they froze to death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pamela Hayden, longtime 'Simpsons' voice actor, hangs up her mic
item.description: “It’s been an honor and a joy to have worked on such a funny, witty, and groundbreaking show,” Hayden said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Unhappy with the 2024 election results? This Italian village is offering $1 homes to Americans
item.description: Americans who are unhappy with the 2024 election results and have fantasized about leaving the country could turn that dream into a reality thanks to a recent offer from an Italian village.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Here's how much Thanksgiving dinner will cost this year
item.description: Here's which Thanksgiving dishes will cost more, and less, this year, according to the American Farm Bureau Federation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Best apps to save and organize your recipes
item.description: How many times have you found a great recipe online only to wonder, "Now, where do I save this?" TikTok and Instagram have built-in bookmark functionality, but these apps not only help you save and organize your collection but also cut through the clutter. Mela, Free to $5, iOS and Mac only.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5
